core.appIs: tic-tac-toe — a two-player m,n,k (3,3,3) game served as a web arena
core.goal: place three of your own marks in a row (horizontal, vertical, or diagonal); otherwise draw
core.howToJoin: take a seat by submitting the first move for a side; the first mover on each side is seated (X moves first, then O); later arrivals are observers

myAffordances: make a move on X's turn to an empty square

expectedPositionTokenSource.note: Sd off => no /profile; the only source is the board.

notes: A=1 renders move forms only for the caller's currently-legal squares (seat+turn-gated) — they appear and disappear as play proceeds. Availability is intermittent, but the SOURCE is still the board: the position labels (TopLeft..BottomRight) are on the rendered board (square aria-labels) regardless of whether an active form is shown. So positionTokenSource=board is correct, not guess. The A=1 wrinkle is execution, not sourcing: an unseated arrival has no active form to submit, so taking the first seat is harder (haiku landed 0 accepted moves here) — but that is a play/execution barrier, separate from where the position tokens come from.
